questions: marshland ar swamp er difference ki?
answers: Wetlands are areas where land is saturated with water, either permanently or seasonally. They include marshes, swamps, bogs, and floodplains — incredibly biodiverse ecosystems.
Wetlands holo emon jayga jekhane mati pani te bhorti thake. Marsh, swamp, bog — shob wetlands. Era prithibir shobcheye biodiverse ecosystem gulor moddhe ek.
Wetlands act as natural water filters, flood buffers, and carbon sinks. They support thousands of plant and animal species and provide resources for millions of people worldwide.
Wetlands are disappearing three times faster than forests. Protecting them is crucial because they filter water, reduce flooding, store carbon, and support enormous biodiversity.
